name: Smoked Chicken & Pepperoni Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the vegetables: Cut half of them into larger pieces (about 1/2 inch) and finely chop the rest using a food processor.
2. Prepare the meats: Use two smoked chicken breasts, pulled from the bone, skinned, and cut into bite-sized pieces by hand. If you don't have smoked chicken, you can use up to 1/4 teaspoon of liquid smoke. Cut one stick (about 12 inches) of pepperoni into moderately thick slices (about 1/8 inch).
3. In a cast iron Dutch oven, dry fry the large cut onions over medium-high heat for about 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Add the remaining vegetables and cook for another 5 minutes on medium heat, stirring frequently.
5. Stir in the spice mixture and incorporate it well with the vegetables.
6. Add a tablespoon of dry roux and stir to combine.
7. Pour in the chicken broth and pepperoni slices. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for at least half an hour or until the vegetables are tender and flavors have melded together.
8. About 5 minutes before serving, add the chunks of chicken breasts to the mixture. At the same time, add more roux if desired to thicken the sauce.
9. Serve in bowls over plenty of rice.
10. For additional smoke flavor, wrap the chicken bones and skin in cheesecloth and place them in the pot with the pepperoni during cooking. Remove all bones and skin before stirring in the chicken pieces.
Makes 8 to 10 servings.
